content:
Now that Zerocopy has been merged, is the plan to eventually replace kernel::transmute with it?

I am contemplating converting nova-core as it would get rid of a lot of our unsafe statements, but want first to confirm that doing so won't interfere with any ongoing effort.

Also this could arguably be a reasonably good first issue. At the same time there are subtleties and we would need this to be done reasonably quickly.

Thoughts?
Upon further inspection, is it actually not trivial to do properly, notably because of the bindings. Do we have a story for making generated binding types implement FromBytes/IntoBytes?
I think you add the derive for the struct using rust/bindgen_parameters
Do you mean, on a per-type basis?
thanks - this probably doesn't make the conversion a realistic Nova good first issue then
We can derive both traits simulatenously for now?
Ah, none of the bindings currently derive anything. And we cannot use zerocopy's derive because it needs #[zerocopy(on_error = "skip")] annotation to bypass trivial_bounds
Yes, so Alice suggestion to derive per-type is probably the one that makes the most sense so far. It is also doable, I'm thinking about a plan
I think we should just patch out the only_derive_is_allowed_to_implement_this_trait restriction and do a unsafe impl for now
There might be the occasional odd type that requires that, but most of the generated bindings should be fine with a derive as they don't have implicit padding
Being able to remove all these unsafe impls is just too good not to try it

Gary Guo said:

Ah, none of the bindings currently derive anything. And we cannot use zerocopy's derive because it needs #[zerocopy(on_error = "skip")] annotation to bypass trivial_bounds

Use the new most_traits derive instead: #zerocopy > Silent derives and `feature(trivial_bounds)` @ 💬
In kernel zerocopy is v0.8.50 which doesn't have this
